title: Bike Touring Vlog - Austria - Leichtenstein Towards Vienna. 5-2010

transcript.text: Hello, video blog. 428. Yeah, it's 428, 730 at night. Mom would say to take this off.
So, I'll do a little video blog. I can't remember the last time I did one. I think it would have been maybe in Emden.
I'm in Austria, about 20 miles east of Innsbruck, 4 kilometers east of some town called something or other.
4 kilometers east of a town called Swats. I did not ride today. Today was my first day not riding because I blew out my knee last night.
I rolled into Innsbruck. I didn't leave until like 420 yesterday because I was spinning all day. I bought this map and I was spinning all day plotting out my route on it.
And then putting tape all over it so that I can have it for 5 months because I got a map of all of Europe.
And yeah, I should do a video of it and do a video of my route on the map tomorrow.
But yeah, I went into Innsbruck and there's this really hot girl at some campground.
But they wanted like 30 bucks, like 20 euros. 20 euros is 30 bucks because it's $1.39 to the euro right now.
And I was like, whoa, that's too much. So, I went into Innsbruck, checked it out. It was pretty.
And then I rode through Innsbruck like an hour more or so.
And then I found this campsite, which is only 7 euro, which is the cheapest one I've seen so far.
So, I hung out here today.
Yeah, because like the last hour of last night, my left knee was in so much pain that I couldn't even put any pressure on it at all.
I had to do all the writing with my right leg.
You know, and even right now, I couldn't walk up stairs.
Called my mom and I guess if that happens, you're supposed to take pain relievers because that takes the inflammation down every 4 hours.
And I do have pain relievers, so I have a leave.
So, I'm going to take those every 4 hours today.
And I probably, you know, tonight is the full moon.
I was going to do a 24-hour bicycle ride, but I can't because of my knee.
And I thought, well, maybe I should do it tomorrow, but I don't think I will.
I think I might stay here tomorrow too, basically is what I'm saying.
Take two full days off of writing.
This is something that I knew was going to happen.
I just didn't know exactly when.
But one thing that I do know from experience is my knee goes out, and then right after that, my leg speed comes into play.
So, I'm excited that my leg speed and my legs are going to kick into gear here pretty soon, which is going to be fun because I can go a lot faster.
And my belly is still there, but it's kind of going away.
I'll do a video of my belly tomorrow.
I think my lungs are starting to come into play.
I do have a little bit of a frog in the back of my throat, which causes me to cough.
And I think the frog is there from all the smoking and also all the inhaler.
The inhaler, like, feels like the inhaler melts away all the phlegm in my lungs, which causes me to cough.
So, it's kind of this, like, balancing act I have to do.
Um, so, um, yeah, I still have asthma.
Like, I'm having an asthma attack right now.
Fuck, I shouldn't have done that.
So, hopefully my lungs will get into gear.
Um, I don't know how long it'll happen, but I'm not going to be able to hammer 100% until my lungs do.
But, whatever.
Um, I, um, I, uh, my legs are actually also, um, sore.
Right now, they're sore.
Um, and so this is the first day.
This is the second full day I've had off of riding.
You know, I haven't been riding, like, having full day riding.
It's only, like, 50 miles a day or whatever.
But, this is the, but I have only had one full day off before this.
It was when I got my, um, my, uh, axle fixed in Cologne, Germany.
So, that was, like, a while ago.
Um, and my, my legs haven't been, um, fresh since then.
So, it's definitely time to let my fresh, my legs completely recover.
Um, you know, if I had an intelligent coach in my soul right now, the coach would be saying,
Yeah, let your legs recover, don't ride.
Um, yeah, so, um, um, yeah, but this, my left knee, this is always, I mean, I've had to cut two bike, bike tours short before, um, because of the same exact problem.
So, it's definitely, um, something that happens.
Um, but Bashar was saying that all I have to do is detoxify my body and then I'll become perfectly healthy.
So, that's, that's part of the bike touring idea for me is to detoxify my body.
I did kind of a new experiment as far as diet today.
I went to the supermarket and bought, um, I had to walk five minutes to some town, get to the supermarket and buy some, um, some food.
I just got a bunch of, like, vegetables and stuff.
I'm going to see if I can get, you know, get a lot of energy on vegetables.
It seems like the vegetables fill me up if I just eat raw vegetables.

So, when I go to the supermarket from now on, you know, instead of going to a, what I like to do is instead of going to, like, a restaurant to buy a meal there or, you know, or buy, like, meat and bread,
Like, go into a place and just buy, like, peppers and, um, like, spinach and, um, you know, fruit and stuff.
Just, like, fruits and vegetables and just eat that raw, just eat it and see if that gets me going.
Because that's pretty much the purest, um, food that you could get, um, and eat raw.
So, um, and we'll see if that goes.
Because, you know, it doesn't seem like I've eaten that much.
It's a lot cheaper, too, and it doesn't seem like I've eaten that much and I'm, like, totally full.
So, so, yeah, maybe if I can detoxify my body with all the sweating and stuff, I could make it so that, you know, I don't have the knee problem again.
I don't think I will have the knee problem again, though.
It usually only happens once, you know, after about a thousand miles.
Right now I have 1136.3 miles on my leg, so that's 936.3 miles on this trip so far.
Um, so, yeah, after about a thousand miles, um, or I guess 1100 miles, 1136 miles, here my knee goes bad.
Um, um, it's just like, like, with the pivot inside my knee, where the pivot, the ball is inside the pivot is where it hurts.
It just got inflamed from too much movement.
Um, and, um, I don't think, you know, it's not from an injury or anything or abuse as a kid.
I actually took a lot of time off when I was a kid.
I was really easy on my legs.
I didn't run for, like, six years, and I didn't ride bicycle bike racing when I was 13 and all that stuff.
Quit ski racing when I was 13 because of Ozgut's slaughter, but this has nothing to do with Ozgut's slaughter.
This is just bad knees.
Um, so, um, yeah, I called my mom, and she's like, well, you should just take the bus to Vienna.
And I'm like, no, I'll just hang out here because it's cheap here, and I'm a purist, so I don't want to start taking buses.
I, um, you know, I'm here to ride.
And that's the main thing is to ride, and then see the sights is actually the secondary thing, so.
Um, and, um, so, um, I, uh, that's what I'm doing.
I think I might stay here tomorrow, and then the day just zoomed by for me because I was tired also.
I slept, so, um, and I don't think my legs are going to be totally fresh tomorrow either, so that'd be cool.

Like, if I go the day after tomorrow, my legs will be fresh, I'll be able to stretch tomorrow because I didn't even have time to stretch because I slept, and then I ate, and then it's like, where did the day go, you know?
Um, and I did roll into here at, like, 12.30 or something last night, so I kind of slept in today, too, so.
Um, I think I will sleep fine tonight, but if not, I'll make some videos.
Um, but it was cool.
Um, I was in this tent laying down, you know, naked, um, with my, uh, check it out, with my, um,
My ventilation flaps open, and, um, I wasn't too hot.
It was cool, you know, in the sun, directly in the sun, so.
But, you know, we aren't in, I mean, it's still ski, ski season, ski season until the end of April, two more days, so.
I even saw a guy, like, carrying skis in boots the other day, so.
Um, so, yeah, there you go.
There's my, there's my, my, my blog for now, okay, my vlog.
Okay.
